Title: G7 finance ministers meet in Paris to overcome their differences
Subhead: The G7 Finance Summit convenes in France on Monday and Tuesday, as US President Donald Trump shatters the founding principles of multilateralism and free trade that have defined this club of wealthy nations.

'Staggering' Iran toll drives up global executions to their highest since 1981, says Amnesty International
Subhead: In 2025, at least 2,707 people were executed globally and 2,159 of these cases were in Iran, Amnesty International said on Sunday.

Title: Ligue 1: PSG defeated by Paris FC ahead of Champions League final
Subhead: Paris Saint-Germain lost 2-1 on Sunday ahead of the Champions League final on May 30 against Arsenal. Coach Luis Enrique said 'it has been a tricky end to the season' for PSG as they prep for 'the biggest match in our history.'

Title: New book by Canadian garbage collector-anthropologist becomes social phenomenon
Subhead: The author and garbage collector, who juggled waste collection work with higher education studies, has written a book about his experience.

Title: Captured, imprisoned and tortured: Russia 'ignores all laws and does whatever it wants' to Ukrainian civilians
Subhead: Since the 2022 invasion, Moscow has been arresting citizens from the occupied territories en masse in an effort to crush all resistance. More than 16,000 people are currently imprisoned, according to the Ukrainian Parliament Commissioner for Human Rights.

Title: Elon Musk criticizes Christopher Nolan for casting Lupita Nyong'o as Helen of Troy
Subhead: The billionaire accused the filmmaker of 'defiling Homer' by choosing a Black actress, claiming Nolan only did so to comply with diversity requirements and win an Oscar. The controversy echoes the Trump administration's opposition to affirmative action policies.

Title: How Corsica's schools became the epicenter of a chess revolution
Subhead: In Corsica, chess players began learning the game since they were children, with the island counting 16 times more registered players per capita than the rest of France.
